question.text: A short put (put writer) position has:

answer.text: Maximum profit limited to premium received and maximum loss equal to (Strike Price - Premium)
Unlimited profit potential and limited loss
Both profit and loss are unlimited
Maximum loss limited to premium paid and unlimited profit

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Short Put (Put Writer): - Maximum Profit = Premium received (when option expires worthless, S >= X) - Maximum Loss = Strike Price - Premium received (when underlying falls to 0) - BEP = Strike - Premium
**The Trap:** Short put loss is NOT unlimited (unlike short call). It is capped at X - P because stock can't go below zero.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Put writing is less risky than call writing due to the bounded downside.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Short put: Max profit = premium. Max loss = X - P. BEP = X - P.
